Halchal Industries - ML Pricing Engine Dashboard

Visualisation of all 6 Power BI CSV datasets | Generated from Python | Open in any browser

Page 1 Page 2 Page 3 Page 4 Page 5 Page 6 Bonus

Page 1: Seasonal Demand Weights

Key Insight: Peak demand: August (Kharif 1.28) is 64% higher than trough month May (Summer 0.78). This directly drives the highest AI prices in Jul-Sep.

Page 2: State Adoption Index (Map)

Key Insight: Gujarat ranks #1 nationally with 7.2 lakh ha under micro-irrigation. Assam is last at 0.2 lakh ha. The adoption index drives a price spread of +6.3% (Gujarat) to -9.6% (Assam) in the current model.

Page 3: Logistics Factor by State

Note (version diff): Every 100km from Pune adds approximately +0.46% to logistics cost. Assam at 2600km incurs a +12.2% surcharge. Note: the current production model (ml_api.py) is ex-factory — logistics is buyer's responsibility. This CSV represents an earlier model version.

Page 4: Price Waterfall (How Price is Built)

Note (version diff): The waterfall shows the step-by-step price build for 16mm Inline shipped to Gujarat in Kharif. Base Rs.1050 -> Final Rs.1140 after all 6 factors. Note: the current model uses demand sensitivity 0.35 (not 0.25 shown here) and smoothing 50/50 (not 55/45).

Page 5: Weight Justification Table

Key Insight: Green shading = factor pushes price up. Red shading = factor is a discount. All weights are sourced from NABARD, NCPAH, and NIC LEADS reports.

Page 6: Zone Price Comparison

Key Insight: Zone 2 (Gujarat) shows the highest AI premiums: +15.8% on 16mm Online, +17.3% on 20mm Online. Zone 5 (Bihar) shows near-zero premium despite higher logistics, because low adoption offsets the freight cost in the price formula.

Bonus: Adoption Factor Price Effect by State

Note (version diff): Adoption coefficient 0.06 in this CSV is from an earlier model version. The current ml_api.py uses 0.15, producing a larger +-10% adoption band across states.
Halchal Industries Final Year Project — ML Pricing Engine Dashboard — Generated with Python (matplotlib + seaborn) from Power BI CSV datasets
